coaching.title: Easy 9 Hilly — Peak Week
coaching.message: Today is the flagship easy run inside your hardest week of the block: ~9 miles on hilly terrain, ~2,000 ft of vert, strictly Zone 2 (target 130-148 bpm, well under your 165 LTHR). 'Easy' is the assignment, not the exception — power-hike anything that pushes your HR toward Zone 3 and keep every climb conversational. This mimics the relentless rolling grind of the Julian Alps, where controlling effort on runnable climbs is what preserves your legs for the back half. If you're running in Buenos Aires, seek out the Costanera or park loops with repeatable rises to stack vert without a single hard surge.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: This is a peak-load week, so fuel it like one. Pre-run: 40-60g carbs 60-90 min out (toast + banana + honey, or oats). On a 90+ min hilly session, practice race fueling now — 60-80g carbs/hr via gels or chews, sipping every 20 min so your gut is trained, not just your legs. Post-run: hit the 30-min window with ~30g protein plus 60-80g carbs to refill glycogen. Across the day aim high on total carbs to support the week's volume, and front-load hydration with electrolytes given Buenos Aires' dry winter air.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: The hardest week only works if recovery keeps pace with load. Non-negotiable tonight: 8+ hours of sleep — you flagged extra sleep for this week and this is where adaptation actually happens. After the run, 10 min of easy walking to flush the legs, then foam-roll calves, quads, and glutes for the climbing load. Legs up the wall for 5 min before bed. If anything feels sharp rather than tired, back off tomorrow rather than digging the hole deeper.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: 63 days out, deep in Peak, the hardest week of 22 — this is the fitness that shows up on the start line in Kranjska Gora. The discipline today isn't going hard; it's the harder skill of holding back on the hills when your body wants to push. Every controlled climb is a rep at the exact patience the Julian Alps will demand. Train the restraint now and race day feels rehearsed, not improvised.
